map.comment: Get the latest content for the cdm instance
Get all active relations
Get the instance

sequence.comment: If it's new, there might not be content yet, create an empty object
sequence.label: !currentContent

map.comment: Get the primary key field

throw.description: ="Could not find primary key field for: " + instance/typeId
throw.label: !primaryKeyField
throw.message: Could not find primary key field

map.comment: Instantiate actual instance
Set the primary key value
Set the booleans to maximize the data gathering for an initial sync
Get all subscriptions for this type and the given systems
If we haven't resolved it yet, get the primary key field
map.label: !primaryKeyField

map.comment: Get all the subscription instances for this particular cdm instance. We also need a stable date for later comparison to prevent additional unnecessary pulls.
map.label: subscriptions

sequence.comment: If we have a filter, check that we are interested
sequence.label: subscription/filter != null

map.comment: Drop master documents
map.label: input/skipMaster

map.comment: If we have no pull interval, set to 5 minutes. By default we don't want to overpoll systems and want to be able to push content. You can manually set it lower if necessary
map.label: subscription/pullInterval == null

map.comment: Get the current instance subscription and the correct relations

sequence.label: input/force != true && subscription/pullInterval != null && instanceSubscription/pulled != null

map.comment: Set pull to false if we are not yet at the window

break.comment: If we pulled in the past and its not decoupled, we _should_ have content? If we don't, skip
break.label: !pull && instanceSubscription/content == null

map.comment: Check that we don't need to skip it
map.label: input/skipPullForSystem != null && pull

break.comment: We have determined that we don't want to pull but there is no content, nothing to see here.
break.label: instanceSubscription/content == null && !pull

map.comment: Get the remote data
map.label: true

map.comment: Otherwise, we take the previous version
Update the ids
map.label: newExternalIds

sequence.comment: If we have no partial instance, we can't do much
sequence.label: partialInstance == null

map.comment: If we had content before but now don't have any, we decouple it
map.label: instanceSubscription/content != null

sequence.comment: Only update our system if we actually pulled data
sequence.label: pull

map.comment: Update relations
Marshal the data
If it did not exist yet, create it. Note that even in the first pullFromRemote for a system this may already exist because of batch pulling
map.label: !instanceSubscription

map.comment: Otherwise, we update it

break.comment: If we don't want to merge the content, continue with the next system
break.label: input/metadataOnly

map.comment: If we have never pushed before, we skip master document because we want to gather as much info as possible. Initial masters can still apply
map.label: instanceSubscription/pushed == null

map.comment: If it is a new subscription (we have never pushed before) and we have an initial master, use that
map.label: instanceSubscription/pushed == null && subscription/initialMasterTypeId != null

map.comment: Get a list of all the properties that are allowed by initial master. These values will WIN even if CDM already has a value for them
map.label: instanceSubscription/pushed == null && subscription/initialMasterTypeId != null

sequence.comment: If we have a master type, let's limit our impact to that. Let's also figure out if the system changed fields beyond that
sequence.label: subscription/masterTypeId != null

map.comment: Diff full pull against the root, we want to see if you changed something beyond the fields you are master of
Limit to the subtype
Diff the partial against the root, don't take the primary key of the cdm into account
We don't want to "unset" values, especially useful during initial merge where data may be fragmented across systems initially
map.label: input/skipNulls

map.comment: If we have never pushed to a system, we assume it can not (intentionally) be in sync with CDM. If we have not explicitly defined an initial master to deal with the situation, we assume existing data in the cdm is MORE correct than the additional system. We only retain new data, not update existing data.
map.label: !newInstance && instanceSubscription/pushed == null

sequence.comment: We have a full diff as well, let's compare our master diff
sequence.label: subscription/masterTypeId != null

map.comment: Retain all the diffs in the full diff that are not in the localized diff
Distinguish between array insertion/deletion and plain update diffs (no longer the case!)
Add all diffs! Check documentation for array approaches
If we have array insertion/deletion, we update the cdm immediately so future array changes are detected correctly. Other changes are combined into a bigger changeset
map.label: arrayDiffToApplyImmediately

map.comment: Map original BEFORE we mask it (link order!). We need this original to find any actually applied differences.
Mask the cdm instance before we apply diff so we can see diff vs original
Patch all the changes together AND in order
map.label: cdmDiff

map.comment: If we have subscriptions, they were updated
map.label: instanceSubscriptions

sequence.comment: If we have any changes, let's set a new content as well
sequence.label: output/changes

map.comment: If we are at the first version, always persist if we have any changes
Do an additional diff to make sure changes do not cancel each other out

sequence.comment: Previously we always pushed it if there were _any_ changes but it is possible that changes cancel each other out. Make sure there is an actual delta. This does assume however that the cdm instance is not modified on the fly!
sequence.label: finalCdmDiffs

map.comment: Deactivate any active instance contents
Create a new instance content
Map the output
Map the stopped for the sync
